name: POLICY-BACKLOG-12
title: coding-rules.md 'Surgical Changes' ambiguous on staged-but-not-committed orphans
description: coding-rules.md section 3 says 'Remove imports/variables/functions that YOUR changes made unused in the current (uncommitted) edit.' This is binary (uncommitted vs committed) but staged-but-not-yet-committed edits sit in between. If an agent stages files, then a later edit orphans something staged earlier in the same session, the rule is unclear: is the staged work still 'uncommitted' (clean it up) or already 'previously committed' (leave it)? Suggest clarifying that 'uncommitted' includes both staged and unstaged work from the current agent session. Location: .claude/features/policy/coding-rules.md section 3 lines 44-48.
